2606.28455.title: Event-Conditioned Diagnostics of Kinematic, Contact, and Object-Permanence Fields in Passive Object-State World Models
2606.28455.paper_summary: World models can predict future physical states, but prediction accuracy alone does not explain how physical information is organized and used inside their latent dynamics. We introduce a controlled diagnostic protocol for studying event-conditioned latent physical structure in passive object-state world models. The protocol tests whether hidden representations encode event-regime information, whether event contexts reweight non-exclusive physical field readouts, and whether field-aligned representational components have functional consequences for prediction. Using a balanced controlled-generator dataset with free-motion, collision, and occlusion events, we evaluate recurrent, attention-based, and latent state-space transition models under a fixed-horizon forecasting setup. The models learn useful predictive dynamics and their hidden states support reliable event-regime readout. Event contexts systematically reweight kinematic, contact, and object-permanence field readouts: free motion is kinematic-dominant, collision combines kinematic and contact structure, and occlusion combines motion-related and object-permanence structure. Time-aligned and directional-consistency analyses further show phase-related shifts in field emphasis. Finally, fixed-horizon projection causal field effect (CFE) shows that suppressing field-aligned directions can degrade event-relevant prediction, with strongest evidence for contact-aligned structure in collision-contact windows and more qualified evidence for object-permanence-aligned structure in hard-occlusion hidden windows. These results support event-conditioned organization and fixed-horizon functional sensitivity of latent physical fields, while not implying explicit physical modules, isolated causal circuits, or context-invariant sliding-window generalization.
2606.28455.paper_summary_zh: 世界模型可以預測未來的物理狀態，但僅僅依賴預測準確性並不能解釋物理信息在其潛在動態中是如何組織和使用的。我們介紹了一種受控診斷協議，用於研究被動物體狀態世界模型中的事件條件潛在物理結構。該協議測試隱藏表示是否編碼事件範疇信息，事件上下文是否重新加權非排他性的物理場讀取，以及場對齊的表示成分是否對預測有功能性影響。使用一個平衡的受控生成數據集，包含自由運動、碰撞和遮擋事件，我們在固定視野預測設置下評估重複、基於注意力的和潛在狀態空間轉換模型。這些模型學習有用的預測動態，其隱藏狀態支持可靠的事件範疇讀取。事件上下文系統性地重新加權運動學、接觸和物體持久性場讀取：自由運動是運動學主導的，碰撞結合運動學和接觸結構，遮擋結合運動相關和物體持久性結構。時間對齊和方向一致性分析進一步顯示了場強調的相位相關變化。最後，固定視野投影因果場效應（CFE）顯示，抑制場對齊方向可能會降低事件相關的預測，碰撞接觸窗口中對接觸對齊結構的證據最為強烈，而在硬遮擋隱藏窗口中對物體持久性對齊結構的證據則較為有限。這些結果支持事件條件組織和潛在物理場的固定視野功能敏感性，同時並不暗示明確的物理模塊、孤立的因果電路或上下文不變的滑動窗口泛化。
2606.28455.author: Yang Liu et.al.
